title: India: Sankla Buildcoon to Invest ΟΔβÄö¬Ι15,000 Cr in SAF Project in Maharashtra
content: Sankla Buildcoon has signed an MoU with the Maharashtra government to invest ΟΔβÄö¬Ι15,000 crore in a Sustainable Aviation Fuel project at Chalisgaon, marking a major step toward aviation decarbonisation, energy security and rural economic growth. Through its clean energy platform Sankla Renewables, the company will develop a large-scale SAF manufacturing facility using agricultural residues such as sugarcane bagasse, cotton stalks, soybean husk and pigeon pea waste to produce aviationΟΔβ²§βÄ‰grade sustainable fuel with globally certified technologies. The MoU, formalised at the World Economic Forum in Davos, highlights MaharashtraΟΔβ²§β³Δs push for transitionΟΔβ²§βÄ‰aligned industrial investments and marks Sankla BuildcoonΟΔβ²§β³Δs strategic expansion from real estate into longΟΔβ²§βÄ‰term clean energy infrastructure. The project will be commissioned in phases through 2029, designed as a longΟΔβ²§βÄ‰life, infrastructureΟΔβ²§βÄ‰grade asset aligned with IndiaΟΔβ²§β³Δs decarbonisation and energyΟΔβ²§βÄ‰security goals. It aims to meet global ESG standards with 100 percent renewable energy use, closedΟΔβ²§βÄ‰loop water systems, electricΟΔβ²§βÄ‰vehicleΟΔβ²§βÄ‰based logistics and zeroΟΔβ²§βÄ‰liquidΟΔβ²§βÄ‰discharge operations, while creating over 3,000 direct and indirect jobs and reducing openΟΔβ²§βÄ‰field residue burning.
